intro: Lotononis genistoides, commonly known as the Lotononis tree or yellow lotononis, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to southern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits, such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lotononis genistoides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
